title: Board view, how to get the current board's ID
excerpt: Is the only way to get the current board ID via the URL when you’re using an IFRAME app for a Board View feature?  Just couldn’t find an official way to know which board your view is running within other than parsing the…

posts.author: deepdev (Chris Douglass)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is the only way to get the current board ID via the URL when you’re using an IFRAME app for a Board View feature? Just couldn’t find an official way to know which board your view is running within other than parsing the boardId from the IFRAME app’s URI.

Just asking bc if I’m parsing boardId and it’s not official means to get the current board ID, then it could possibly change one day and break my app. Thanks

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
It should be in the context.

Apps Framework

monday.get

You can use the monday.get software development kit (SDK) method to retrieve data from the parent monday.com application where your app is currently running. This object can only be used in client-side apps. Parameters type : The type of requested...

This will always give you the details you need to know about where your app is running.

posts.author: deepdev (Chris Douglass)
posts.cookedHtml: hmmm, i see. i’ll look. so the correct way to find out what board your app is running on, etc etc is always in the context… makes sense. thanks!!
